mnemonics: HARMS: High Absorption, Removes calcium, Mottles teeth, Skeletal fluorosis - to remember the effects of excessive fluoride.
keyPoints: Fluoride occurs naturally in groundwater and can exceed safe drinking water limits (1.5 mg/L WHO guideline).
Dental fluorosis (mottling of teeth) and skeletal fluorosis (painful bone disease) are the primary health effects of chronic excessive fluoride intake.
Defluoridation techniques, such as using Activated Alumina or Nalgonda technique (with alum), are essential for treating contaminated water.
commonMistakes: Assuming all fluoride in water is artificially added, when it often occurs naturally from geological deposits.
Believing fluoride is only harmful to teeth; chronic exposure also severely damages bones and joints.
